title: salix alba var. subintegra
intro: Salix alba var. subintegra, also known as white willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix alba var. subintegra include its ability to alleviate headaches, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Salix alba var. subintegra include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
